title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During the investigation into the disappearance of Thomas Braid, Jonah Lincoln provides an account of his activities on the night of the crime. Lincoln states that he met Mrs. Braid and Helen Braid for dinner at Longchamps and subsequently took them to the Park Theatre. He claims he returned to his office to work on accounts with an auditor, though he later clarifies that the auditor left at eight o'clock, leaving him alone. Lincoln, the Brad women, and the victim took the Long Island train and a taxi home, arriving around one o'clock. Lincoln notes they saw nothing unusual and specifically mentions they did not look at the path leading to the gazebo because it was dark. Mrs. Braid explains she only discovered Thomas was missing the following morning when Fox woke them.

The inquiry shifts to personal histories and connections. Lincoln reveals he has an unnamed sister, Hester, who resides on Oyster Island, where a man named Harleot has established a "Sun Cult" involving nudism. Mrs. Braid discloses that Thomas was Romanian and had a previous wife in Europe. The discussion turns to Meagal, a Greek amateur explorer who has been sailing on his steam yacht, the *Helen*, for almost a year; Helen Braid suggests Meagal went to the South Pacific. Lincoln notes that Thomas and Meagal had been in the carpet import business together for sixteen years and were financially stable, suggesting money was not the motive. When Ellery questions if religion is a factor, Lincoln points to the nature of the crime—nailing someone to a cross. Mrs. Braid adds that while Thomas was baptized in a Greek Orthodox church, he was considered an atheist who did not believe in rituals.
